name: La Selva del Camp - Reus - Constantí - La Pobla de Mafumet – return through the Camp de Tarragona
name_original: La Selva del Camp - Reus - Constantí - La Pobla de Mafumet – vuelta por el Camp de Tarragona
region_intro: Hiking around La Selva Del Camp offers diverse terrain situated between the plains of Camp and the foothills of the Mussara mountain range. The region is characterized by mountainous landscapes, including the Prades mountains, and features the Riera de la Selva river. Puig d'en Cama, at 718 meters, is the highest point within the municipality, providing elevated views. This area combines natural beauty with historical elements, such as ancient hydraulic architecture. Best hiking trails around La Selva Del Camp The most popular hiking route is Puig d'en Cama Loop Trail, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route ascends to the municipality's highest point, offering panoramic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Circular al Puig d'en Cama pel Camí del Rec, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail highlights the region's hydraulic heritage, including historical water channels. Local hikers also love the Camino de la Coma por la Ermita de Sant Pere del Puig — circular desde Selva del Camp, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 19 minutes. Hiking around La Selva Del Camp is defined by the Mussara and Prades mountains, the Riera de la Selva, and historical hydraulic structures. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in La Selva Del Camp are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore La Selva Del Camp's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in La Selva Del Camp?
A: La Selva Del Camp offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 routes available. These range from easy walks to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, La Selva Del Camp has 9 easy hiking trails perfect for beginners or families. These routes often feature gentler terrain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the area.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around La Selva Del Camp?
A: The terrain around La Selva Del Camp is diverse, situated between the plains of Camp and the foothills of the Mussara mountain range. You can expect mountainous landscapes, including access to the Prades mountains, and areas featuring the Riera de la Selva river. Some routes also highlight historical elements like ancient hydraulic architecture.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For experienced trekkers seeking a challenge, La Selva Del Camp offers 5 difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309090555/puig-de-la-torre-and-gr-172-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig de la Torre and GR 172 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 17.6 km with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in La Selva Del Camp?
A: Yes, many routes in La Selva Del Camp are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952486846/puig-d-en-cama-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which ascends to the municipality's highest point, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307701219/circular-al-puig-d-en-cama-pel-cami-del-rec" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular al Puig d'en Cama pel Camí del Rec&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the region's hydraulic heritage.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking?
A: Hiking in La Selva Del Camp offers access to several natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can reach &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/597960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama (717 m)&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the municipality, for panoramic views. Other notable features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/880628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foradada Rock&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/880629" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Goi Natural Arch&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any caves or unique geological features to explore?
A: Yes, the region boasts interesting geological features, including several caves. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/265083" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cave of the Cranes&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/725611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Avencs de la Febró&lt;/a&gt;, a popular hiking destination featuring a beautiful narrow canyon.

Q: Is La Selva Del Camp a dog-friendly hiking destination?
A: While specific dog policies can vary by trail, many natural areas in Catalonia are generally welcoming to dogs on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage and ensure your dog is well-behaved and under control to respect wildlife and other hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in La Selva Del Camp?
A: La Selva Del Camp offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for hiking. Summers can be hot, especially at lower elevations, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are advisable during this season. Winters are mild, making it possible to hike year-round, though higher elevations might experience cooler temperatures.

Q: Are there hiking routes that highlight the region's historical or cultural heritage?
A: Absolutely. The region has a rich history, and some routes highlight its hydraulic heritage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307701219/circular-al-puig-d-en-cama-pel-cami-del-rec" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular al Puig d'en Cama pel Camí del Rec&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features historical water channels, the Pont Alt (or Pont dels Moros) medieval aqueduct, and remnants of old flour mills.

Q: How can I access the trailheads, and is parking available?
A: Many trailheads in La Selva Del Camp are accessible by car, and parking is generally available in or near the town center and at common starting points for popular routes. It's advisable to check specific route details on komoot for parking information relevant to your chosen trail.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in La Selva Del Camp?
A: The hiking trails in La Selva Del Camp are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of natural beauty with historical elements, and the well-maintained paths that cater to various ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Mussara or Prades mountains?
A: Yes, La Selva Del Camp is situated at the foot of the Mussara mountains and close to the Prades mountains, so many routes offer spectacular views of these ranges. Ascents to points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/597960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig d'en Cama (717 m)&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent vantage points over the surrounding mountainous landscapes.
tips: Church in Constantí with an architecture that combines the baroque and modernist styles. It is made up of three naves and a bell tower that was built later.
Nice religious building of Gothic and Renaissance style, located in the Selva del Camp. Currently, the space is used as a restaurant.
The Castillo de la Selva, also known as the Paborde castle, is located in the town of La Selva del Camp. There are also remains of the medieval wall. Its construction dates from the 12th century.
